positives: Five consecutive years of documented prior insurance history (2020–2024)
Premium base is relatively stable across available years, ranging approximately $580,805 to $630,843
Low claim frequency in 2020 (2 claims) and 2023 (3 claims)
Maintained coverage with recognized national carriers (Markel, Zurich, Chubb, EMPLOYERS)
concerns: Policy was cancelled by Zurich Insurance Group in 2022
Claim frequency is volatile and elevated: 9 claims (2021), 6 claims (2022), 11 claims (2024)
2024 shows highest claim count in the 5-year window (11 claims) with no corresponding premium or carrier identified
No carrier identified for 2024, suggesting possible mid-term placement, binder-only status, or data gap
No premium data available for 2024 policy year
Loss ratios not disclosed for any policy year despite column existing in record
Total of 31 claims across 5 years with no reserve or paid loss dollar amounts provided
Carrier turnover every year across all 5 years may indicate difficulty placing or retaining coverage
risk_highlights.loss_history_summary: Five-year prior insurance history (2020–2024) across three lines of coverage (WC, GL, CP). Bundled premiums and claim counts by year: 2020 — EMPLOYERS Insurance Group, $580,805.53, 2 claims, no cancellation; 2021 — Chubb Limited, $630,843.20, 9 claims, no cancellation; 2022 — Zurich Insurance Group, $607,294.45, 6 claims, CANCELLED; 2023 — Markel Corporation, $619,820.26, 3 claims, no cancellation; 2024 — carrier unknown, premium unknown, 11 claims, no cancellation. Total reported claims: 31. No loss dollar amounts, reserves, or loss ratios provided. Cancellation by Zurich in 2022 is a material underwriting flag.

extracted_text_sections.claims_narrative: Claim counts reported by year without dollar loss data: 2020: 2 claims; 2021: 9 claims; 2022: 6 claims (policy cancelled by Zurich Insurance Group); 2023: 3 claims; 2024: 11 claims (carrier not identified). Aggregate claim count over 5 years: 31. No paid losses, incurred reserves, or per-claim detail provided. Frequency spike in 2024 (11 claims) warrants further loss runs and carrier explanation.
